capabilities: Ensure comprehensive model lineage is recorded in the registry for traceability and reproducibility.

outputs.name: the authorship information by appending the names

procedure.action: Assert all input data by checking that each dataset used in training is accompanied by a unique identifier and checksum; log discrepancies to lineage_validation_log by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Compile lineage data by aggregating model metadata, including hyperparameters, training metrics, and evaluation results, into a structured JSON object; ensure all fields are non-null using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Register the compiled lineage data in the model registry using the registry's API; confirm successful registration by checking for a 201 Created HTTP response status using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Log the authorship information by appending the names and contact details of all contributors to the model lineage record; ensure compliance with data privacy regulations using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Verify the integrity of the registered lineage by comparing the stored checksum of training data against the original dataset checksum; alert if mismatches are detected using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Procedure to record complete model lineage in a registry, including training data, hyperparameters, metrics, evaluation results, and authors.
title: Comprehensive Model Lineage Recording

failureConditions: Traceability is compromised when lineage data lacks unique identifiers, causing difficulties in reproducing model results.
Reproducibility fails when hyperparameters are not logged, leading to inconsistent model performance across environments.
Data privacy violations occur when authorship information is improperly logged, causing unauthorized access to personal data.
metrics: all input data by checking that each dataset used in training is accompanied by a unique identifier and checksum
